item.dc:creator: LunaAtlas

item.title: The Unbearable Lightness of Being Frank
item.description:
Got home, Frank started telling me about his day
Some of you have asked how Chessie and Puddles are doing with Frank. Well....
There are bad days
And there are good days.
Honestly, Puddles is really chill with Frank and actually tried to groom Frank for a little bit. Frank was not okay with that though. But sitting near each other is okay. Chessie is going to take some time to warm up to him. That's okay. It's only been five days.
Frank is Frank.
Bonus video. @KittensForDays reminded me that I have a Ring setup in my house as well. Here's Frank having a blast bouncing off the furniture. Going to see Superman tonight. It should be good, I've heard good things about it. Hope your Thursday evening is going well.
item.dc:creator: FrankTheKitten

item.dc:creator: ItHappenedInThe20thCentury

item.title: Native Voices
item.description: Long before digital preservation or cultural funding, two women quietly reshaped the course of Native American history. Ethnomusicologist Frances Densmore, working in the early 20th century, recorded more than 2,000 tribal songs on wax cylinders using rudimentary phonograph technology. Her work documented ceremonial chants, healing songs, and oral histories from dozens of tribes—many of which were at risk of being lost to colonization and cultural suppression. Across decades, another guardian emerged: Gladys Tantaquidgeon, a Mohegan elder and traditional medicine woman. She chronicled her tribe’s ancient remedies and beliefs in handwritten notebooks. Though stored away for years, these records proved crucial when the Mohegan Tribe sought federal recognition in 1994—a process requiring deep proof of continuous cultural identity. Her documentation helped restore sovereignty and inspired a cultural renaissance marked by the founding of the Mohegan Sun. These acts of preservation were more than academic—they were acts of resistance. In a world that sought to erase Indigenous cultures, these women safeguarded what mattered most: language, ceremony, and memory. Their legacy remains a cornerstone of historical preservation and Indigenous resilience.
item.dc:creator: TheTaoofRob

item.title: The Harpy's Meme Library, Volume: 2 (scream time)
item.description:
Hello and welcome to another meme dump, my imgur friends. I cannot for the life of me remember what number I was on previously before I nuked my account, so I guess we're starting all over and this is now number 2 (again). Anyways, behold, funny images!
I love my dog and he is a very good boy... but he is also very food motivated, so I would absolutely get abandoned for that pizza crust. RIP me.
*
*
*
*
*
*
*
*
*
*
*
*
*
*
*
*
*
*
*
*
*
*
I missed cropping the dumb watermark out of this one. I apologize for my failure, you may shame me.
*
*
*
*
And then the cycle repeats itself yet again
*
*
ah yes, a delicious can of end me
*
*
*
*
*
I'm something of a raccoon myself
*
*
*
*
*
*
AAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAA
*
*
*
All righty, that's all for this dump my imgur friends. Until the next one, don't forget to be awesome.
item.dc:creator: SecretHarpy

item.dc:creator: abbiistabbii

item.title: Appreciating your parents afterwards ....
item.description:
Good parents love to make their kid's lunches ...
More weird than funny to me. How many times did this happen?
That is so Dad.
Amen to this. And the taking of pictures along the way. Memories for a lifetime.
Yeah ... that is a weird flex if you ask me.
Yep, I know that feeling. So great to be entrusted with your kids' friends and making sure they get home safely. The other parents certainly appreciate you.
item.dc:creator: Herebychoice

item.title: The is sounding even better now...
item.description: https://www.axios.com/2025/07/10/superman-movie-immigrants-fox-news-james-gunn According to the article "Gunn described the story as one of "[a]n immigrant that came from other places and populated the country, but for me it is mostly a story that says basic human kindness is a value and is something we have lost." Not sure how else you would describe Superman as anything other than an immigrant, but that is enough for the right-wing influencers have a conniption fit and scream "Superwoke". Samething happened with the Barbie movie, and that turned out to be a really great movie. So looks like I am seeing this in theaters...
item.dc:creator: Selfinflictedwounds

item.title: I have always wanted to make my own single-line shirt designs so today I have begun my quest.
item.description: This is my first concept. The design itself is one non-intersecting line. That will be my main theme. Wish me luck! Once I start committing these concepts into reality, I will host them on my new IG account. Even if you never want to get my designs, any support to my account would be appreciated https://www.instagram.com/tee.foust?igsh=bjRqcTUxMHh2eGpz
item.dc:creator: tfoust10
